text.text:
Epoch Coffee has a unique, no-frills charm that makes it a great spot to work or just enjoy a quality cup of coffee. On Fridays, it feels more like a co-working space, with most customers focused on their laptops. This isn’t necessarily a downside or a plus—just an observation. The calm background music and quiet atmosphere definitely help with concentration.

The space itself is simple and functional, with an open kitchen, a bar counter, and multiple seating areas. Every table has an outlet, making it an ideal place for remote work. There are also two outdoor seating areas for those who prefer fresh air.

The interior isn’t modern or overly designed, but that’s part of its authenticity. It feels like a place that gained popularity long ago and has remained a staple thanks to its steady flow of visitors. The friendly staff, relaxed vibe, and consistently good coffee make it a solid choice for both work and casual visits.

text.text:
Barrett’s Coffee is a wonderfully cozy and peaceful café, perfect for those who appreciate a quiet, focused atmosphere. On a Sunday visit, we noticed people engaged in their own worlds - some working on laptops, others reading books. In fact, several people were reading, which says a lot about the kind of space this is. It’s the kind of place where concentration and calmness feel like the norm.

The coffee is excellent, and the service is warm and welcoming. A standout feature is their separate roasting room, housing two massive coffee roasting machines along with stacks of coffee bags, hinting at large-scale roasting operations. Whether they only serve their coffee in-house or also sell it elsewhere, it’s clear they take their craft seriously.

This is a must-visit for anyone who appreciates coffee not just as a drink but as a culture. Barrett’s Coffee doesn’t just serve great coffee - it cultivates an environment that truly embraces the coffee experience. Highly recommended!

editorialSummary.text: European-style cafe serving tea, coffee, English baked goods, savory pies and French-style quiches.

text.text: Went to Brentwood Social House for a friend’s birthday and tried their afternoon tea experience ($50 per person). We had a big group, and they did a great job accommodating us. The setup was super cute—pretty tea cups and pots that really added to the vibe. I had Earl Grey, which came with milk and honey or syrup options. The tea itself was simple but nice, and it just felt good to sip tea and hang out with friends. The real star was the snack tower. Each person got two pieces of cucumber sandwich, goat cheese with fig jam sandwich, and chicken salad sandwich—all super tasty and well-balanced. Then there was a second tier with marmalade brownies, tea cookies, and mini cupcakes, which were all sweet and delicious. The top tier had raisin biscuits with whipped butter and jam—classic and comforting.Honestly, it was way more food than we expected. Everyone ended up taking leftovers home in boxes. The whole experience felt fun, cozy, and a little fancy in a casual way. Definitely a unique and enjoyable way to celebrate something special with friends. Would totally recommend it if you're looking for something a bit different.

The top tier had raisin biscuits with whipped butter and jam—classic and comforting.Honestly, it was way more food than we expected. Everyone ended up taking leftovers home in boxes. The whole experience felt fun, cozy, and a little fancy in a casual way. Definitely a unique and enjoyable way to celebrate something special with friends. Would totally recommend it if you're looking for something a bit different.

text.text: I usually give a place more than one shot before I write my not very good opinion but this time I’m disappointed again. I’m not a difficult or snobby person but when I go to a coffee shop with decent reviews I expect at least a decent service. Ordered a coffee and was asked if it’s for here or to go and I said “for here” assuming it means it will be served in a mug. (I don’t take my hot drink in a plastic cup). They gave me a buzzer and almost 30 minutes later I walk in to check if it’s ready but I did not see what I thought I ordered. I walked over the play room inside and decided to wait there and several minutes later I heard a very faint beeping and no buzz on my “buzzer”. There were only 2 of us in that room and there is no way I would have heard that faint beeping if the playroom was full of kids. Maybe was worth mentioning that the buzzer does not vibrate?   I would have walked around with a toddler a backpack and a buzzer close to my ear to not miss it Idk. I walk over to the counter and found my order in a plastic cup already cold. It’s ridiculous for a cup of cappuccino to take this long, the place was not even that packed. I did not want to make a scene and did not complain but I visit very many coffee shops and my simple drink never takes this long to get ready despite the number of customers. Kids play room was a chaos and had pieces of trash left behind from customers before us I guess. I started picking up the trash so my child does not touch it. But my cold Cappuccino served in a to go cup paired with insanely slow service on both my visits and dirty toys are some of the reasons I won’t come back.

text.text:
I have to say, this place is truly amazing. One of the highlights is how family-friendly it is. Inside, there’s a cozy little area perfect for younger toddlers, complete with a play kitchen, toy tools, and a sweet selection of books. It’s such a thoughtful setup that keeps little ones engaged and happy. Love it.

Outside, there's a great playground area in the shape of a ship, complete with two slides. It looks fantastic and adds to the charm, making it a wonderful spot for kids to run around and explore while parents relax nearby.

While I didn’t try the coffee, the tea was excellent, very smooth, and flavorful. The food was also a standout. We came on a Sunday morning, and although it was bustling, the staff was clearly in sync and working hard to make sure everything ran smoothly.

The curry pie was especially delicious, rich in flavor, and perfectly done. You can tell a lot of care goes into everything they serve. If we’re ever back in town, this will absolutely be a place we return to.

text.text:
They share their space and identity with a yoga studio, and that’s important to know upfront. It’s not just two businesses coexisting - they’re part of the same operation, and some of the reviews you’ll find reflect the yoga experience. This can be confusing for anyone expecting a dedicated café experience. We arrived based on high ratings, thinking it was a standalone coffee shop, only to discover that it’s primarily a yoga space with a coffee component.

The café itself is pleasant, with good coffee and a relaxing atmosphere. There’s calming music, some seating, and a focus on a mindful vibe that aligns with the wellness theme. But as a coffee destination, there’s nothing particularly outstanding here - just pretty good coffee.

Flora still earns 5 stars for quality and ambiance. But if you’re mapping out coffee spots to explore in Austin, this likely isn’t a place you’d want to prioritize. It’s more of a serene corner to enjoy a drink if you’re nearby, but not a coffee shop in the fuller, standalone sense.

text.text: Meh just a big ol’ bummer. My favorite coffee shop in my home town carried Merit coffee before they closed three years ago, I was excited to experience it again in Austin. That was until I arrived. The only thing I wanted was a cup of the signature drip coffee. The (I assume) shift lead at the time told me they were out and he made the decision to not brew any more for the day, and wasn’t interested in making more. I thought this was odd since they were packed and it was three hours before close. So I pivoted to their new Subzero frozen cold brew, where he proceeded to beat the machine for the last remnants of the batch to barely fill the cup. Disappointing experience, for a coffeeshop with so much hype, train your people better.

text.text: Located near Highland Collective, this coffee shop is truly the most adorable little coffee truck. The owners (white guy/Japanese woman sorry didn’t grab their names) who runs this truck are so sweet and so kind. They have an outside play area with a playscape for kids with picnic tables that are shared with two other food trailers and it’s nice they also have some tables next to their space as well. It’s also shaded which is nice and they have electric fans that are running all over. Parking is easy and free as you can park in Highland Collective parking lot which is right next door. To find the truck it’s off Airport Blvd and you will see it behind a grey chain linked fence. Drink wise I got their seasonal drink which was mango matcha drink with oatmilk was absolutely delicious - the mango was fresh and the matcha was great, not chalky at all and great flavor. The barista had made a little too much matcha and gave me the extra matcha which was sweet. My husband got the Miso Piloncillo and liked it as well. I appreciate the drink isn’t too sweet at all. For their hours as of 6/16/25 Yelp is incorrect. It is Wednesday - Friday  9 AM - 3 PM and Saturday/Sunday 8 AM - 3 PM (subject to change) but that’s what they told me. We went on a Sunday and it was great! Highly recommend and can’t wait to go back to try their banana drink!

I have been going to this place since it was opened. I have tried coffee from all over the world, and I have to say this is hands down, the best coffee shop in all of Austin TX and some of the best coffee I have had in my life. All in a cute little kei van.

Many of the drinks are inspired by Asian flavors.  Kyoko serves matcha, coffee, and other seasonal and specialty drinks.  I highly recommend dreams of ube, matcha latte, and the miso piloncillo which is a show stopper. None of the drinks are excessively sugary, and  they all have very complex and deep flavors. It is not too rich or overwhelming either. Just right!

Patrick is extremely talented and creative when it comes to making coffee and matcha.  He goes out of his way source  high quality beans and matcha. Higher quality than your average coffee shop.  He is extremely knowledgeable as well and happily explains the menu items in detail.

I have to say, this might be Austin's cutest coffee, but it's also Austin's best coffee.

text.text:
Great place, gets busy quick

Mozart’s Coffee Roasters is a true Austin gem, offering a perfect blend of scenic views, great coffee, and cozy vibes. Nestled on the shores of Lake Austin, this spot provides a peaceful escape from the city, with a spacious lakeside deck that’s ideal for studying, catching up with friends, or enjoying a solo coffee break. The menu features a wide range of coffee drinks, house-roasted beans, pastries, and even light meals. Their cold brew and cappuccinos are especially popular, and the pastries—particularly the almond croissant—are always fresh. During the holidays, their famous light show transforms the place into a magical destination. The Wi-Fi is strong, seating is plentiful, and the ambiance—complete with live piano music on some nights—makes it more than just a coffee shop. Whether you’re a local or a tourist, Mozart’s is a must-visit for anyone looking to experience the charm and flavor of Austin.

This is my favorite outdoorsy coffee place in Austin — and hands down my favorite waterfront coffee spot. The lake views are stunning, especially at sunset, and the outdoor seating makes it perfect for relaxing and a must go if you’re visiting the city.

This place is an Austin classic for a reason. Whether you’re a local or visiting, Mozart’s is a must-stop—especially if you’re into waterfront views, Great coffee, and cozy vibes that hit year-round.
Located right on Lake Austin, the spacious deck is perfect for sipping your favorite drink. It’s also one of my go-to spots for getting some work done or just enjoying a peaceful moment outdoors.
There’s plenty of patio seating with gorgeous views from every angle, and the best part? It’s dog-friendly, so your pup can relax right alongside you.
Their coffee? Always on point. They roast their own beans in-house, and the variety is huge—whether you’re into bold espresso, sweet seasonal drinks, or something iced to cool off in the Texas heat. They also have a variety of pastries, desserts, and light bites to keep you fueled.

What makes it even better:
•	There’s always live music or an event happening
•	Paddle board yoga on the lake
•	Outdoor movie nights
•	Rentals if you want to get out on the water

Bonus: During the holidays, Mozart’s transforms into a magical light show that’s 100% worth braving the crowds.

text.text:
I would put Epoch (at least this location!) in the top tier of coffeeshops in Austin. It fits the bill perfectly: fantastic drinks served by really friendly and personable baristas, plenty of comfortable seating with great access to outlets, and just overall good vibes regardless of whether you're there to work or relax.

Drink recommendation: I'm probs pretty basic in saying this but Iced Mojo (treat yourself to the 'full' version rather than the 'half' version). You basically can't go wrong with any of the teas, but I particularly like the iced peach/passionfruit (?) tea they've had recently.

Note: It's pretty close to campus (+ it's awesome) so it tends to get packed. There's a large patio outdoors that usually has space, especially in the heat these days. Sometimes I'll wait out there to get some vitamin D until space opens up inside.

text.text:
What a cute coffee shop! Located inside yoga studio (Yoga retreat) and opened for about a year this coffee serves some majorly great coffee. My husband who is a coffee snob loved his pour over Ethiopian coffee (for the real coffee extraordinaire they use a V60 for it - don't ask me what that means). They have different pour overs from different countries and some fermented beans whatever that means. I got the horchata latte decaf and that was delicious and thankfully to me not as sweet as an horchata so I really enjoyed it - a little horchata but not overly sweet and the espresso helps cut through it. And thankfully was non dairy which I appreciate. The barista was super nice and I saw him greeting everyone and having convos with people that came in.

The seating area is tiny (maybe 12 people could sit at most?) but has very comfy chairs and great spot to catch up with a close friend or maybe even a first date you're not sure about. There are about 6 seats with a high long table if you need a table to study.  They do have WiFi. Also the shop smells amazing - I think because it's tied to yoga studio but it smells like nature and very zen.

Parking is free with it being located in a strip mall so plenty of parking all around.

Food wise they offer pastries from La Patisserie they get each morning - we got the cronut which was good. They also have tacos from El Dorado but do sell out. They also have a small grab and go fridge with apples, Chobani Greek yogurt and kombucha.

text.text: I visited Terrible Love for the first time a few weeks ago and was not disappointed! The atmosphere is industrial yet organic, airy, and so peaceful. In addition to the atmosphere and friendly employees, the menu is simple yet creative - I tried the grapefruit brown sugar latte and was so pleased! Not ingredients you would think to pair together but it was fantastic!! Can't wait to try the other seasonal specials. Plenty of parking and pet friendly as well. Looking forward to my next visit!

text.text: Kyoko Coffee is absolutely delish and particularly darling! The coffee is fantastic and watching it be made creates a unique experience that helps you to appreciate the taste even more. The owner, Patrick, is incredibly knowledgeable of national and global coffee culture and you can visually see (and taste!) the intentionality in each cup. Pour-over is wonderfully smooth with an aroma of berries accompanying you, and the umami mocha is that perfect “not too sweet” for those who are chocolate addicts ^-^ highly recommend!

text.text: This place claims to be Austin's cutest coffee and it really lives up to the name! It has a really interesting menu with lots of coffee and matcha options, and the owners are so sweet. They took their time explaining the drinks to us helping my friend and I pick out our krdert (Ube matcha latte and miso piloncillo coffee) and both were absolutely delicious. I'd been meaning to come try this spot for a long time and am glad I finally made it, and am looking forward to my next visit and making this a regular spot in my coffee shop rotation. There are also a couple of other food trucks nearby and a few benches under a covered patio to sit and sip.

text.text: Kyoko Coffee is one of those rare gems that instantly feels like home. Tucked away on Airport Boulevard next to Highland ACC, it’s become one of our favorite pit stops. The owner is a true standout, not only does he make some of the best coffee in town, but he’s also genuinely kind and always a pleasure to chat with. Whether you’re taking a break from work or just passing through, this spot is a breath of fresh air. The coffee is rich, smooth, and full of character, the kind that reminds you why you fell in love with coffee in the first place. It’s not just another coffee shop, it’s a community. Highly recommend stopping by.

text.text: There’s a reason this place is an Austin staple. Live music on Sundays, the best coffee, amazing selection of drinks and food, pastries are delicious, atmosphere is amazing, plenty of seating, great views, and right along a bus route for easy access if parking get too full. Only cons are that it can get really busy so you may have to wait for a table or in a long line to order, there aren’t a lot of outlets if you’re trying to study there, and there’s lots of bees near trash cans so you may have to beware of where you’re sitting. But, there’s more pros than cons so they’re not dealing with breakers. The coffee is seriously good: lattes are perfectly sweet, iced cappuccino is so smooth, tea lemonades are refreshing, and smoothies are perfectly blended. Come here and I don’t know how you’ll regret it!

text.text:
Great place, gets busy quick

Mozart’s Coffee Roasters is a true Austin gem, offering a perfect blend of scenic views, great coffee, and cozy vibes. Nestled on the shores of Lake Austin, this spot provides a peaceful escape from the city, with a spacious lakeside deck that’s ideal for studying, catching up with friends, or enjoying a solo coffee break. The menu features a wide range of coffee drinks, house-roasted beans, pastries, and even light meals. Their cold brew and cappuccinos are especially popular, and the pastries—particularly the almond croissant—are always fresh. During the holidays, their famous light show transforms the place into a magical destination. The Wi-Fi is strong, seating is plentiful, and the ambiance—complete with live piano music on some nights—makes it more than just a coffee shop. Whether you’re a local or a tourist, Mozart’s is a must-visit for anyone looking to experience the charm and flavor of Austin.
